LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANANYA PATTANAYAK'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT - THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. AN APPLICATION FOR RELIEF FROM ENTRY OF DEFAULT MADE UNDER CCP SECTION 473(B) SHALL NOT BE GRANTED UNLESS IT IS "ACCOMPANIED BY A COPY OF THE ANSWER OR OTHER RESPONSIVE PLEADING PROPOSED TO BE FILED THEREIN." Notice Of Uncontested Hearing On Plaintiff Chaitanya Ramavajjala'S Request For Court Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Charles Crompton, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings reported by: Irene L. Tofft, CSR#12913.

Jesse C. Ralph, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, CHAITANYA RAMVAJJAALA.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) ANANYA PATTANAYAK, aka ANANYA M. PATTANAYAK, aka ANANYA PATTANAYAK NARANG, aka ANANYA NARANG.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.

Plaintiff Chaitanya Ramavajjala sworn and examinded on his own behalf.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

Damages: $181,000.00
Pre-Judgment Interest: $22,306.80
Attorney's Fees: $0.00
Costs: $1,557.92
Other: Punitive damages: $10,890.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $215,754.72
Plaintiff recovers against Defendant on his Intentional Tort [Fraud] caue of action as set forth in his Complaint filed/endorsed May 14, 2014. Please see Attachment 7.
Judgment signed in open court.

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JESSE C. RALPH, TEL. NO. 776-1826 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARC LIBARLE, TEL. NO. 928-2400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ANANYA PATTANAYAK'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT - The motion to set aside entry of default is denied without prejudice. An application for relief from entry of default made under CCP section 473(b) shall not be granted unless it is "accompanied by a copy of the answer or other responsive pleading proposed to be filed therein." (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312). Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm
